channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Apple gives Siri its own dedicated app
item.description: Siri is finally getting its own app.

item.title: Apple is fixing the headache of splitting the bill with its new Siri in Camera feature
item.description: "If you're grabbing a bite with friends and point your iPhone at the bill, then [you can] select what you ordered to split the tab with Apple Cash," said Apple VP of Software Sebastien Marineau-Mes.

item.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Ready to Get Personal
item.description: From a standalone app to a Google Gemini partnership, here’s everything you need to know from WWDC 2026 about Apple’s upcoming overhaul of Siri.

item.title: How I benchmarked a 100% local RAG pipeline to 9/9 (zero API keys)
item.description: Most "chat with your documents" demos work in an afternoon. Then you hit the last 20%: retrieval that misses the right passage, an LLM that confidently makes things up, a reranker that wrecks your...

item.title: Building a Startup Is Not Just About Having an Idea - Here's What Nobody Tells You
item.description: There's a narrative going around right now that goes something like this: Come up with an idea. Describe it to an AI. Ship a startup. Profit. I get why it's appealing. Tools like Lovable, Bolt,...

item.title: Caching a Huawei SUN2000 over Modbus for Home Assistant
item.description: My Huawei SDongle accepts exactly one Modbus connection at a time — but Home Assistant, my AC·THOR and evcc all want to read the inverter at once. Polling it from three clients just gave me...

item.title: The perfect background music for Vibecoding...
item.description: While vibecoding, you sometimes need some background music. But music can also be a massive distraction. A summary of my journey in finding the perfect background tune. I started with rap, then...

item.title: 65 million Brazilian companies — public data you can use right now
item.description: Brazil has one of the most open business registries in the world. The Receita Federal (Federal Revenue) publishes the complete CNPJ database — 65+ million companies — as public data. Here's what...

item.title: Mock Interview Experience - Part 2
item.description: Here is my second mock interview experience, where I got an idea of how to learn each concepts and how to understand in different perspective. It was so helpful for me to learn more and grow more....

item.title: A Platform Where Messages Self-Destruct After a Minute, Yeah I Made That :)
item.description: Have you ever had a thought you just needed to get out of your head, but also didn't want it sitting around forever? That idea stuck with me for a while, and a lot of platforms inspired me to...

item.title: Apple’s long-awaited AI Siri overhaul is finally here
item.description: The idea behind the new "Siri AI" is to turn the assistant from a voice controlled assistant into an AI companion that can do a lot more.

item.title: CI/CD with GitHub Actions & Sharding (Playwright + TypeScript, Ch.21)
item.description: A suite that only runs on your laptop protects only your laptop. This chapter wires the whole thing into GitHub Actions: spin up the dockerized SUT, run the tests sharded across parallel machines,...

item.title: Reporters & Observability (Playwright + TypeScript, Ch.20)
item.description: A suite is only as useful as what it tells you when it fails. A red X with no context means a re-run; a red X with a trace, a screenshot, and the environment it ran against means a fix. This...

item.title: Apple is redesigning Screen Time and overhauling child controls
item.description: At WWDC 2026, Apple announced an overhaul to its Screen Time parental control features that aim to improve its safeguarding features to protect children who use iPhone, iPad, and Mac devices. Some...

item.title: Parallelism & Flake Control (Playwright + TypeScript, Ch.19)
item.description: A fast suite is a parallel suite — and parallelism is where flakiness is born. The good news: we've already met (and fixed) the main culprits in this course. This chapter names the model and turns...

item.title: Amazon is launching AI-generated custom merch
item.description: Amazon is expanding its print-on-demand features to AI-generated designs created using Alexa for Shopping for products like T-shirts, water bottles, and hoodies. Shoppers can use text prompts to...

item.title: Apple announces iPadOS 27
item.description: Today at Apple's Worldwide Developers Conference, the company announced new features coming to the iPad with iPadOS 27 including optimizations such as apps launching up to 30 percent faster by...

item.title: The UK Is Betting on a Billion-Dollar AI Supercomputer to Kick Its Addiction to US Tech
item.description: The British government thinks a state-backed infrastructure initiative will help supercharge homegrown chip startups.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more
item.description: Apple’s WWDC 2026 event kicked off this morning at 10 a.m. PT at Apple Park, starting a week full of expected announcements around Siri, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence and more, along with developer...

item.title: Apple announces Siri AI and its next generation of Apple Intelligence
item.description: Two years after first revealing its plans for Apple Intelligence and a smarter Siri that never fully materialized, at WWDC, Apple just revealed a new set of AI features and a smarter, more...

item.title: Meta Deletes Face-Recognition System From Its Smart Glasses App After WIRED Report
item.description: The code WIRED identified is gone from the latest version of Meta AI, the companion app for the company’s smart glasses. Meta won’t say why or whether it’s coming back.

item.title: Apple announces iOS 27
item.description: Apple announced its next major iOS update, iOS 27, at WWDC 2026 on Monday. Apple is highlighting performance and design improvements, trust and safety upgrades like a Screen Time redesign, and...

item.title: Meta Blocks NSO Group's New WhatsApp Phishing Attack, Files Contempt Order
item.description: Meta on Monday said it detected and blocked spear-phishing attempts linked to Israeli spyware vendor NSO Group. In addition, the tech giant said it's filing a federal court contempt order against...

item.title: Apple announces macOS Golden Gate 27
item.description: Apple has announced macOS 27 Golden Gate, the next version of its desktop operating system. Alongside updates to Apple Intelligence, Siri, search, and parental controls, the new Golden Gate...

item.title: Critical Zcash Vulnerability Found and Fixed
item.description: If you’re a user—owner?—of this cryptocurrency, this is important: On May 29, the security researcher Taylor Hornby found a critical vulnerability in Zcash Orchard privacy pool using Claude Opus...

item.title: WWDC protesters want Apple to ban Elon Musk’s apps
item.description: Apple's big developer conference is today, and protesters are using the occasion to call on the company to remove "nudify apps" from the App Store and pull "known" child sexual abuse material from...

item.title: WWDC 2026 live blog: On the ground at Apple’s keynote
item.description: It's conference season! Today, we're back at Apple Park in Cupertino, California, for Apple's annual developer keynote. Unlike other conferences, there's a distinct rhythm to WWDC. We know we're...

item.title: The Verge’s Father’s Day 2026 gift guide
item.description: With Father's Day on the horizon, happening June 21st, it's time to start thinking about what kind of gifts you want to buy for the fathers you care about. You know your dad best - he may be a man...

item.title: NotebookLM’s Gemini 3.5 upgrade adds a cloud computer and help finding sources
item.description: Google is rolling out "across the board" updates to NotebookLM. The AI-powered note-taking app now uses Google's upgraded Gemini 3.5 model, which will allow it to respond with "more accurate and...

item.title: Amazon now lets you design custom merch using AI
item.description: A new feature in the Amazon Shopping app allows users to generate designs with Alexa, then print them on products like T-shirts, hoodies, and tumblers.
